changeSet.author: openelis-medlab
changeSet.comment: Ensure sequence is past any explicitly inserted IDs

changeSet.author: openelis-medlab
changeSet.comment: Insert predefined medical laboratory workflow page templates (16 pages)

column.name: description
column.value: Register patient and create lab order

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Register patient with demographic data (name, sex, age, contact). Create lab order selecting required tests (CBC, Chemistry, etc.). Link existing patient if already registered.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Patient Registration and Lab Order Entry

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Collect specimens from patient. Record container type (EDTA vacutainer, plain tube, etc.), apply barcode label, record collector ID and collection time. Support multiple sample types per order.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Collection - Phlebotomy and specimen labeling

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Scan sample barcode to retrieve details. Verify sample matches order. Check transport conditions. Accept samples that pass initial inspection.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Reception - Barcode scan and order verification

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Perform sample-type-specific quality checks. Chemistry: check hemolysis, lipemia, icterus, volume. Hematology: check clotting, anticoagulant, delay. Record pass/fail with rejection reasons if applicable.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Quality Control - Sample acceptance criteria validation

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Document transport packaging compliance per IATA PI650. Check primary container (seal, barcode, absorbent), secondary packaging (integrity, watertight), tertiary packaging (labeling, temperature logger). Record compliance status.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Transport Packaging - IATA PI650 compliance documentation

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Route samples to appropriate departments based on ordered tests. Assign priority (STAT/Routine). Create department worklists. Track allocation status.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Allocation - Department routing and worklist generation

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Perform department-specific processing. Hematology: prepare smears, staining. Chemistry: centrifugation, serum separation. Microbiology: inoculation, culture setup. Create aliquots as needed.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Processing - Centrifugation, smears, staining, aliquots

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Assign samples to storage locations using hierarchical selector (Room > Device > Rack > Box > Position). Record storage temperature requirements. Support barcode scanning for rapid assignment.

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Record AM/PM temperature readings for storage locations. Flag excursions outside acceptable range. Document corrective actions for any excursions.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Environmental Monitoring - Temperature logging and alerts

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Generate analyzer worklists. Import results from instruments via CSV/HL7. Match results to samples by well position or sample ID. Flag abnormal values automatically.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Testing - Instrument interfacing and result import

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Review QC results on Levey-Jennings charts. Monitor for Westgard rule violations (1:2s, 1:3s, 2:2s, R:4s, 4:1s, 10x). Enter corrective actions for failures. Track dual-level QC (Normal/Pathologic).

column.name: default_content
column.value: QC Dashboard - Levey-Jennings charting and rule monitoring

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Enter test results manually or review auto-imported results. System auto-flags H/L/C based on reference ranges. Add result comments where needed.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Result Entry - Manual entry and result flagging

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Review results for technical accuracy. Apply 2-level approval workflow (Technician > Supervisor). Request retest if needed. Document approval with electronic signature.

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Generate patient reports with validated results. Print or export to PDF. Track turnaround time metrics. Support electronic delivery to ordering provider.

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Track sample location and status in real-time. View complete chain of custody. Search samples by accession number, patient, or barcode. Monitor sample journey through workflow.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Sample Tracking - Chain of custody and location tracking

column.name: default_instructions
column.value: Document sample disposal with method, facility, date, and operator. Support transfer to biorepository for long-term storage. Verify regulatory compliance. Complete audit trail.

column.name: default_content
column.value: Disposal and Archiving - Compliant disposal and biorepository transfer

changeSet.author: openelis-medlab

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM clinlims.dictionary d JOIN clinlims.dictionary_category dc ON d.dictionary_category_id = dc.id WHERE dc.local_abbrev = 'noteBookType' AND d.dict_entry = 'MEDLAB'

changeSet.comment: Add MEDLAB notebook type to dictionary
changeSet.sql: INSERT INTO clinlims.dictionary (id, is_active, dict_entry, dictionary_category_id, sort_order, lastupdated) SELECT nextval('clinlims.dictionary_seq'), 'Y', 'MEDLAB', (SELECT id FROM clinlims.dictionary_category WHERE local_abbrev = 'noteBookType'), (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(sort_order), 0) + 10 FROM clinlims.dictionary d JOIN clinlims.dictionary_category dc ON d.dictionary_category_id = dc.id WHERE dc.local_abbrev = 'noteBookType'), NOW() WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM clinlims.dictionary_category WHERE local_abbrev = 'noteBookType');
